feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Cannot subscribe to Cloud Identity Service
entry.content:
I tried to subscribe to the Cloud Identity Service in multiple SAP BTP subaccounts, but none of them worked. I also tried using a new email to create a new BTP trial account, but I still couldn’t subscribe to Cloud Identity. I attempted to delete and subscribe again, but that didn’t work either. Switching between the TEST and PRODUCTIVE parameters also had no effect.

I checked the entitlements, and Cloud Identity is included. Also tried remove the entitlement and add it again, result still the same

Has anyone faced a similar issue? Please help me with this.

entry.title: IAS activation email not triggered after 2nd rehire check
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Has anyone come across IAS issues with welcome email not being triggered after 2nd rehire check and ONB is restarted for the new hire? The SF standard ONB emails get triggered, but there is no new IAS welcome email to activate their account after the rehire match. As per this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3585126/E, there should be a 2nd activation email or password reset email of some sort triggered from IAS, but this isn't happening for the client.

I have a High priority ticket with SAP and it's been with their engineering team for a week now with no update, hoping someone here has an answer or can at least point me in the right direction.

Thank you!

entry.title: Adding Learning Content Provider as Application in IAS
entry.content:
Hello,

We are in the process of setting up a new learning content provider in our SF LMS system.  For various reasons for authentication we have been told that the best route to take would be to set the learning content provider up as an application in IAS. 

We are an SSO environment and have SSO configured in IAS.  By setting up the Learning Content Provider in IAS, it would use IAS for authentication when a person is accessing a course.  

I logged a ticket with SAP and I was given this KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002677737 and told that this would be the correct path to take and that we do not need to configure it in IAS. 

I went back to the Learning Content Provider with this information. They have said that while this works, it is very basic and will only send the userID and not information like names and that other customers of theirs have set them up in IAS. 

Just curious if anyone has had this scenario before and what approach they took? 

Kind regards,
Helen

entry.title: Application Frontend Service app with IAS leads to 401
entry.content:
We're building an integration monitoring application on SAP BTP using SAP CAP (Node.js) as the backend and a SAPUI5 Fiori Elements app as the frontend. For deployment, we chose the Application Frontend Service (app-front) as a managed approuter — instead of deploying and maintaining our own approuter module.

Is there any example of Application Frontend Service with IAS or also with your own CAP backend? I don't know what is broken.

For authentication, we deliberately went with IAS (Identity Authentication Service) only — no XSUAA.

This mta.yaml let's you deploy, but will end up with 401 Unauthorized calls to the backend in the browser logs of the deployed frontend app:

 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: cpi-monitoring
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: cpi-monitoring-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    properties:
      AMS_DCL_ROOT: ams/dcl
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
      - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
        parameters:
          config:
            app-identifier: srv
            credential-type: X509_GENERATED
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-cert-url: ${protocol}://${default-host}.cert.${default-domain}
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      instances: 1
      routes:
        - route: ${default-url}
        - route: ${default-host}.cert.${default-domain}
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm-ci
    deployed-after:
      - cpi-monitoring-ams-policies-deployer
  - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres-deployer
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/pg
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      no-route: true
      no-start: true
      tasks:
        - command: npm start
          name: deploy-to-postgresql
  - name: cpi-monitoring-ams-policies-deployer
    type: javascript.nodejs
    path: gen/policies
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
        parameters:
          config:
            app-identifier: ams-policy-deployer
            credential-type: X509_GENERATED
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      no-route: true
      no-start: true
      tasks:
        - command: npm start
          memory: 512M
          name: deploy-dcl
  # - name: cpi-monitoring-app-content # for Work Zone deployment
  #   type: com.sap.application.content
  #   path: .
  #   requires:
  #     - name: cpi-monitoring-repo-host
  #       parameters:
  #         content-target: true
  #   build-parameters:
  #     build-result: resources
  #     requires:
  #       - artifacts:
  #           - monitoring.zip
  #         name: monitoring
  #         target-path: resources/
  # - name: monitoring
  #   type: html5
  #   path: app/monitoring
  #   build-parameters:
  #     build-result: dist
  #     builder: custom
  #     commands:
  #       - npm install
  #       - npm run build:cf
  #       - npm ci
  #       - npm run build
  #     supported-platforms:
  #       []

  - name: cpi-monitoring-app-deployer
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - monitoring.zip
          name: cpimonitoringmonitoring
          target-path: resources/
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
      - name: cpi-monitoring-app-front
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    parameters:
      config:
        destinations:
          - name: monitoring
            url: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
            forwardAuthToken: true
            forwardAuthCertificates: true
          - name: ui5
            url: https://ui5.sap.com

  - name: cpimonitoringmonitoring
    type: html5
    path: app/monitoring
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: postgresql-db
      service-plan: free
  - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        authorization:
          enabled: true
        display-name: cpi-monitoring
        oauth2-configuration:
          token-policy:
            access-token-format: jwt
        provided-apis:
          - description: API exposed by the application
            name: cpi-monitoring-ias-api
        xsuaa-cross-consumption: true
      service: identity
      service-name: cpi-monitoring-ias
      service-plan: application
  # - name: cpi-monitoring-repo-host # for Work Zone deployment
  #   type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  #   parameters:
  #     service: html5-apps-repo
  #     service-name: cpi-monitoring-html5-service
  #     service-plan: app-host
  - name: cpi-monitoring-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: cpi-monitoring-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
  - name: cpi-monitoring-app-front
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: app-front
      service-plan: developer
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

 

 

Destination name mismatch (404 on OData calls)

xs-app.json routes OData requests to destination: "monitoring". We initially had the destination named srv-api in the app-deployer config. Result: every OData call returned 404 with a message like:

Error while retrieving destination monitoring from destination service. HTTP status code: 404

Fix was: rename the destination to monitoring — exactly matching xs-app.json.

401 Unauthorized from CAP backend

After fixing the 404, we got 401 from the backend on every OData request. The app-front debug logs showed:

Exchange token required = false

When I try to change things the deployment often fails like this:

[cpi-monitoring-destination-content] [ERROR] Unexpected error occurred

entry.title: Change User ID in SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

is there any chance to change the User ID of a User in SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication Service) afterwards? 

In UI it is not possible and as far as I can see it is also not possible using the SCIM API (Identity Directory API). But maybe there's someone who has an idea.

Could it be changed by SAP Support?

Any idea is much appreciated.

Best Regards

Tobi

entry.title: "organization name" "50"
entry.content: I understand that the “Organization Name” field in SuccessFactors is limited to 50 characters. However, I have not been able to locate any official documentation (manuals, help pages, data model specifications, etc.) that explicitly states this field length.Could you please provide the location of the SAP official document that specifies:"The maximum length of the Organization Name field is 50 characters"?A URL, document name, or instructions on where to find the relevant material would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Possible attributes for IDP Transformation setting
entry.content:
Hello,

is there any overview available to see what attribute/ values I can select for source system "SAP Application Server ABAP" and for target system "Identity Authentication".

I want to adjust the default transformation to our use and therefor it would be great to know what can be selected and what's the correct name.

 

Best regards

Max

entry.title: IASテナントをSubaccount配下で有効化する構成についてのご質問
entry.content:
いつもお世話になっております。

SAP BTP Identity Authentication Service（IAS）の構成に関して、
以下の点についてご教示いただきたく、ご連絡いたしました。

■ 質問内容
IASテナントをSAP BTPのSubaccount配下に配置（または紐づけ）して
有効化できる、という話を耳にしました。

従来は、IASテナントはグローバルに提供され、
Subaccountとは論理的に連携する形で利用するものと理解しておりますが、
Subaccount単位でIASテナントを有効化・管理できるような構成が可能なのでしょうか。

■ お伺いしたい点
・本構成が実際にサポートされているのか
・実現方法（設定イメージや前提条件の概要）
・当該構成を採用することによるメリット
　（例：運用管理、テナント分離、セキュリティ、ライフサイクル管理 等）

entry.title: SSO Migration from ADFS to Entra ID. IAS – Guidance Request
entry.content:
Hello,

We are planning to migrate our corporate Identity Provider from ADFS to Microsoft Entra ID for SSO authentication. SuccessFactors (including LMS), using IAS. At this stage, we are in the planning phase and would like to begin with the LMS Preview environment before proceeding to Production. 

System / Landscape:

  • Product: SAP SuccessFactors (Learning)
  • Identity Layer: SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS)
  • Current Corporate IdP: ADFS
  • Planning Corporate IdP: Microsoft Entra ID
  • Environments: LMS Preview (Test) and LMS Production

We would appreciate guidance on the recommended approach to configure Entra ID as a new Corporate IdP in IAS alongside the existing ADFS.

Thank you,

Serguei,

entry.title: IAS/IPS tenants they are Neo or CF
entry.content: We have a set of IAS/IPS tenants that we are not sure whether they are Neo or CF. Could you please indicate from the attached list which ones are CF?

entry.title: Banner Name appearing on mobile app
entry.content: We see that banner name in the banner card is appearing in the mobile app of SF. We wish to disable this. How do we do that?

entry.title: Assemble Scrap Not Working for HPA TSP in IBP
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

Assembly Scrap(PDS Header) for HPA TSP is not working, I can refer the SAP help where this attribute marked as Base (Cross-Application). I hope this functionality is available for TSP Planning. Please let me know if we need to enable any specific settings to consider this in the TSP.

entry.title: IAS Configuration in PRD Without RMK
entry.content:
IAS has been configured successfully in the Test environment with SSO, and Recruiting Marketing (RMK) is also integrated. In this setup, internal candidates are routed via IAS and can successfully apply for jobs through the career portal.However, in the Production (PRD) environment, RMK is not currently integrated. Management has requested to proceed with IAS configuration in PRD, but without RMK.Before proceeding, I would like to seek your confirmation on the following:

  • Can IAS be configured in PRD without RMK integration?
  • Will internal candidates still be able to access job postings and apply using IAS in the absence of RMK?

As RMK is a separate module and requires additional time for implementation, along with user approvals, please guide me before initiating PRD preparation.

entry.title: SF creating users in IAS and using HiredDate to fill Validfrom
entry.content:
Hej everyone.  

really struggling with how to get this working.  can anyone spot what we are doing wrong? 

in our SF source transformation we have:

            {
                "sourcePath": "$.hireDate",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validFrom']",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "manipulateDate",
                        "sourceDateFormat": "Date(milliseconds)",
                        "targetDateFormat": "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS"
                    }
                ]
            },

 and in our target transformation we have:

            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validFrom']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validFrom']"
            },

 we know that hireDate is coming across and being filled.  We have used https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-group-employees-based-on-their-hiredate-attribute-via-identity/ba-p/13546869 to assign people to groups based on hiredate and that is working but we can't get the actual value to appear. 

What am i not seeing?  

thanks

sara

entry.title: Cloud ALM User
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on SAP Cloud ALM setup and I am not sure what the best way is to onboard users to SAP Cloud ALM.

My setup:

- Corporate IDP
- IAS as Proxy
- Users are provisioned from the corporate IdP into IAS

Now I am unsure what the best method is for Cloud ALM user onboarding.

As far as I understand, there are these options:
1. CALM User Management App (manual user creation in Cloud ALM)
2. BTP Role Collections (central assignment via BTP Cockpit)
3. IPS group based provisioning

My questions:

What is the best practice in real projects?
What is most commonly used in production?
Are there any known issues or limitations with these approaches?

Thanks for your help!!!

Best Regards

entry.title: Cannot access IAS Admin Console – redirected to /admin/forbidden.html (BTP Trial)
entry.content:
I have a SAP BTP trial subaccount with Cloud Identity Services subscribed.

 

I can access the BTP cockpit and other services (Destinations, SAP Build Work Zone, etc.),
but when trying to access the IAS Administration Console I am always redirected to:
/admin/forbidden.html

 

I am the only user of the tenant and it seems the Administrator role was lost.
There is no option to "Log on with a different Identity Provider".

 


IAS tenant: awbofyup0.trial-accounts.ondemand.com

 

Could SAP please restore an IAS Administrator for this tenant? I could provide whoever is necesary with my subacount name and my user.

entry.title: Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
ARIBA User Schema for Accounting/Shipping Fields, which are not available in IAS/IPS .  What are the SAP delivered solution for user sync from IAS to ARIBA for these additional fields.

 

Cloud Identity Services - Source & Target Systems Transformation schemas mapping fields for ARIBA - Company Code , SAP Plant, Purchase Org, Purchase Group, Cost Center, Delivery Address, Deliver To

entry.title: ARIBA B&I User Provisioning
entry.content:
Have any one implemented Cloud IPS for provisioning the User to ARIBA B&I with additional fields like Company Code, Purchase Org, Delivery To 

I am not able to find these fields in IAS/IPS for populating from SuccessFactors/AD, And i want to understand the end-to-end configuration process for populating this data on to IAS/IPS and provision it to ARIBA periodically. 

Appreciate any help and guidance.

Regards

Mohan

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud to handle bulk posting for movement type 309?
entry.content:
We have a business requirement to transfer multiple batches of different materials within the same plant using movement type 309.

Currently, we need to process this in bulk (high volume), and manual posting is not feasible.

entry.title: How to combine SAP Application Management Services (AMS) with corporate IDP?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

For BTP applications, SAP recommendeds to use AMS for authorisation in future. For this to be possible, users must exist in the Identity Directory of the SAP Cloud Identity Services.

In an enterprise environment, the situation typically looks like this:

  • Employees are managed in the corporate IDP (e.g. Entra ID).
  • The corporate IDP is connected to the SAP Cloud Identity Service
  • A trust exists between the BTP subaccount and the SAP Cloud Identity Services 

In this standard scenario, users are not created in the Identity Directory, but simply routed through SAP Cloud Identity Services. Consequently, no AMS policies can be assigned.

One option would be to use Identity Federation to move users from the Corporate IDP to the Identity Directory. However, in the case of Entra ID, this sync only runs every 40 minutes. Therefore, this is not really a viable solution.

In the past, shadow users were automatically created in SAP Cloud Identity Services, but this is no longer the case and we cannot find an option to enable it.

What is the intended solution? Can AMS not be used in conjunction with a corporate IDP?

Thank you for your response!
